purpose: Refresh the NOOS operational custody self-pin from c7f39ff3 to origin/main a4bdf1f3, clearing the activation-doctor noos_pin_freshness STALE. This is the follow-up custody operation that #77 (Wave 0 SG re-pin) explicitly deferred as out-of-scope 'NOOS self-pin refresh'.
status: SUBMITTED for independent verification (author != verifier; no self-PASS)

verification_before_apply.new_pin_is_origin_main: YES — a4bdf1f3 == origin/main == HEAD at draft time (clean tree)
verification_before_apply.new_pin_ancestor_of_head: YES — git merge-base --is-ancestor a4bdf1f3 HEAD = true
verification_before_apply.artifact_neutral: YES — the custody-carried NOOS artifacts are BYTE-IDENTICAL between c7f39ff3 and a4bdf1f3 (git diff --stat c7f39ff3 a4bdf1f3 = empty) for: noetfield-org/FOUNDER_REASONING_MOTOR_OPERATIONAL_BINDING_v1.md, noetfield-org/schemas/SCHEMA_INDEX_v1.md, and the four motor schemas (founder_reasoning_packet / founder_reasoning_result / motor_job_contract / private_worker_binding v1). The pin advances only over unrelated motor/receipt/CI commits (incl. #77 SG re-pin).
verification_before_apply.artifact_blobs: all 13 custody artifact blob SHAs still verify at their pinned commits (0 mismatches) — re-pin is artifact-consistent
verification_before_apply.sg_pin_untouched_and_correct: SG pin remains dc6080d8 (unchanged). dc6080d8 IS still an ancestor of SG main 49f17594 and matches the canonical sg-authority pointer. Bumping SG to 49f17594 was deliberately NOT done (#77 adjudicated: 49f17594 is post-merge receipt commits ahead; pinning there would desync from the canonical pointer).

noos_repo.commit c7f39ff3 -> a4bdf1f3; previous_commit -> c7f39ff3 (prior live pin); pin_note updated; version 1.2.0 -> 1.3.0 + issued_at refreshed to reflect the new issuance (deviation from #77, which left version/issued_at stale — corrected here for honest metadata)
changes.scripts/noos_claude_activation_doctor_v1: hardcoded NOOS_CUSTODY_PIN c7f39ff3 -> a4bdf1f3 (doctor must agree with pins-file; custody_pins_noos_agree)
changes.CLAUDE.md: authority table custody-chain cell NOOS c7f39ff3 -> a4bdf1f3 (doctor claude_md_custody_pin_current)
changes..claude/noos/AUTHORITY.md: NOOS operational custody pin citation c7f39ff3 -> a4bdf1f3; stale descriptor ('parent of the activation commit') corrected to 'origin/main tip; self-pin refreshed, artifact-neutral'

cleared_by_this_pr: noos_pin_freshness — now PASS (NOOS custody pin == origin/main)
residual_STALE: sg_pin_freshness — SG main 49f17594 vs pin dc6080d8: EXPECTED/BENIGN, out of scope. Adjudicated by #77 (dc6080d8 = ratified authority-landing commit matching the canonical pointer). NOT this PR's concern.

known_post_merge_residual: Once this PR merges, origin/main advances to the merge commit and noos_pin_freshness will read STALE-by-one-commit (its own merge). This is the inherent self-pin-vs-own-merge residual, benign and identical in class to what #77 documented for the SG side. The pin correctly marks the last main commit at which NOOS custody was re-proven; it is not intended to equal a not-yet-created merge commit.
founder_decisions_surfaced: Confirm the self-pin should track origin/main tip a4bdf1f3 (last proven main) — accepting the benign STALE-by-own-merge residual after merge — which matches the doctor's noos_pin_freshness == origin/main check.
Confirm the pins-file version bump 1.2.0 -> 1.3.0 + issued_at refresh is acceptable (#77 left both stale; corrected here). No verifier asserts these fields.

Custody-completeness (pre-existing, out of scope for this pin-advance): a NEW schema noetfield-org/schemas/machine_identity_binding.v1.schema.json (added by canon commit b6c2de3a, within the c7f39ff3..a4bdf1f3 range) now sits in the custody-tracked schemas/ directory but is NOT enumerated in this pins file's artifacts[] (only the 4 motor schemas + SCHEMA_INDEX are). custody_artifact_blobs still passes 13/13 (per-file enumeration, not a dir-tree hash), so the pin is unaffected. Decide separately whether machine_identity_binding should be registered as a custody artifact.
tests: full repo suite 222 passed (python3 -m pytest -q, 4.83s, 2 benign deprecation warnings); JSON artifacts parse; activation doctor exit 0 with 0 FAIL / 55 PASS / 1 benign STALE.
not_done: SG pin bump (deliberately excluded — benign residual, would desync canonical pointer)
